channel.title: Daily Quote — Rev. Randy Lewis
channel.description: A daily quote from the writings and sermons of Rev. Randy Lewis

item.title: Give us the courage and fortitude to do what is in our power to do. May we work toward the greater good, being always mindful of how our actions, or inaction, affect others.
Give us the courage and fortitude to do what is in our power to do. May we work toward the greater good, being always mindful of how our actions, or inaction, affect others.
Give us the courage and fortitude to do what is in our power to do. May we work toward the greater good, being always mindful of how our actions, or inaction, affect others.
“Give us the courage and fortitude to do what is in our power to do. May we work toward the greater good, being always mindful of how our actions, or inaction, affect others.”
“Give us the courage and fortitude to do what is in our power to do. May we work toward the greater good, being always mindful of how our actions, or inaction, affect others.”
“Give us the courage and fortitude to do what is in our power to do. May we work toward the greater good, being always mindful of how our actions, or inaction, affect others.”
"As a minister, I understand the power that words can have. Words can either build up or tear down; they can bring us together or drive us apart."
"As a minister, I understand the power that words can have. Words can either build up or tear down; they can bring us together or drive us apart."
“Give us the courage and fortitude to do what is in our power to do. May we work toward the greater good, being always mindful of how our actions, or inaction, affect others.”
“Give us the courage and fortitude to do what is in our power to do. May we work toward the greater good, being always mindful of how our actions, or inaction, affect others.”
“Give us the courage and fortitude to do what is in our power to do. May we work toward the greater good, being always mindful of how our actions, or inaction, affect others.”
"As a minister, I understand the power that words can have. Words can either build up or tear down; they can bring us together or drive us apart."
"As a minister, I understand the power that words can have. Words can either build up or tear down; they can bring us together or drive us apart."
“Give us the courage and fortitude to do what is in our power to do. May we work toward the greater good, being always mindful of how our actions, or inaction, affect others.”
